title: marketing — helper functions
description: Ergonomic helpers for capturing marketing events, resolving providers, and hashing PII. All helpers are thin wrappers around bound services — never business logic. Callers who need the wire-shape of events use the DomainEventMapper attribute path; helpers here are for imperative flows (support tools, tests, ad-hoc scripts).

helpers.purpose: Imperative capture path. Returns the created MarketingEvent OR null when suppressed by consent OR when no eligible providers exist. Wraps MarketingEventCapturer::captureManually with the current tenant + attribution + consent snapshot. Reserved for support tooling — domain code should use the #[AsDomainEventMapper] pattern.

Applies the provider's PII hashing rules (email lowercased+trimmed+SHA256; phone E.164 normalized+SHA256; name lowercased+ASCII-folded+SHA256). Returns the transformed array. Providers that don't require hashing (GA4, GTM Server, Custom Webhook) receive the raw identifiers. Consumed by MarketingPayloadTransformer implementations.

Returns the driver instance for a provider_config row. Delegates to MarketingProviderManager::forConfig() which reads $config->provider + $config->config and calls the appropriate createXxxDriver factory. Reserved for advanced consumers — typical dispatch flows go through MarketingEventDispatcher.

Returns the active provider_configs for (tenant, event_type) — is_active=true AND event_type IN enabled_event_types AND circuit_breaker_open_until IS NULL OR <= now(). Used by MarketingEventDispatcher AND by support tooling that pre-computes the fan-out plan before dispatching.

Builds the { signature, timestamp } pair used by CustomWebhookProvider + GtmServerProvider. Signature = SHA256(secret + timestamp + payload). timestamp defaults to time() when omitted. Consumers add these to request headers (X-Marketing-Signature + X-Marketing-Timestamp for custom_webhook; X-Gtm-Signature + X-Gtm-Timestamp for gtm_server).

Convenience wrapper over AttributionResolver::forSubject($subject)->snapshot(). Returns the frozen attribution shape ready for insertion into marketing_events.attribution jsonb. Reserved for support tooling that pre-computes marketing events from historical data.

Returns true when the config is in test_mode AND the provider supports test mode AND the caller has marketing.providers.test permission. Used by the SDUI test-provider button + the CLI test-provider command.

notes: Helpers deliberately delegate — testing swaps the bound service to a stub, and every helper follows.
No helper directly touches the DB — everything routes through repositories or services. Keeps the boundary clean.

The capture_marketing_event helper is intentionally NOT the primary capture path. Production code registers #[AsDomainEventMapper] classes; the helper exists for support scripts + one-off backfills + tests.
